![][image1]

# ***Project overview and requirements report***

The Software Readvanced project is a Progressive Web Application (PWA) designed to support students studying the NSW Software Engineering course. The app is aimed at providing easy access to learning resources, study materials, and interactive content to help students better understand key concepts of the course. 

This project will feature sections such as:

* A homepage to navigate through different sections of the website  
  * The homepage will contain a landing page to  greet the user  
  * The About section provides users with information about the app.  
  * A contribution to the PWA section \- this project aims to be open source to allow others to provide information or suggestions/feedback if they have any issues.  
* A learning resource section containing notes, guides, and tutorials.  
  * More resources can be added by opening a pull request to the GitHub repo.  
* Security \- Login system using local databases and a hashing system to protect data.

## Software and tools used:

* Frontend: React \+ Vite for building and serving the user interface (client) to the user.  
* Backend: Flask (Python) with SQLite (sqlalchmey for sql queries) for handling user accounts, API endpoints and data.  
* Development tools:  
  * [Node.js](http://Node.js) and npm for frontend dependency management (React.js)  
  * Python (Flask) for backend logic  
  * Git and GitHub for version control and future collaboration.  
  * (Jetbrains) PyCharm and WebStorm as the main IDEs for development.

## Hardware Used:

* Development device:  
  * A Windows laptop with a full coding environment, used for writing code, running the local server, and debugging the application.  
  * Testing device \- A second Windows laptop with nothing except for Python installed to run the application on the client

## Measures for Success:

| Functionality | The app loads correctly on multiple devices, with working navigation and resource section (can be tested using browser viewport tools) |
| :---- | :---- |
| Responsiveness | The user interface adapts smoothly to different screen sizes (desktop and mobile) |
| Usability | Students can easily access and use resources without technical issues or confusion. |
| Performance | The PWA runs with minimal lag and passes basic Lighthouse performance checks. |
| Authentication | Students can log in and out of their client using their local account username and password. |
| Offline support | The app can load cached pages and assets when offline. |

# ***Design process.***

Are Storyboards not for videos, animations? \- websites should use ui design tools or wireframes to determine the look and feel of the design.

**User interface (UI) and user experience (UX) inspiration:**

* Example designs that I found cool: [Showcase \- Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com/showcase)   
  * All made with Tailwind CSS  
* I reviewed the Tailwind template for various UI components (hero, section, navigation, etc.) and drew inspiration from it, deciding to create my own versions in Figma. This approach would also allow me to customise them later on when actually coding.  
  * [Tailwind CSS Components \- Tailwind Plus](https://tailwindcss.com/plus/ui-blocks#product-application-ui)  
* I also received inspiration from JetBrains website (font was amazing, good choice):  
  * [jetbrains website](https://www.jetbrains.com)  
* ![][image2]  
* The design is amazing. I took inspiration from this section and added it to my homepage's about section.

# ***Algorithms , desk checks and structure charts***

### Pseudocode:

* System design \- pseudocode will be used to design and describe  how the backend will talk to client  
  * **Pseudocode will be simplified but will still extensively explain how the backend system logic works for user registration. (page limit is 30\)**  
  * Frontend (client) will not contain any algorithm design as frontend does not contain algorithms but instead requires different methods of development   
    * In this case using figma as a ui design tool.

For Auth system:

* This algorithm will be in charge of handling login/signup requests and account creation  
* The user will be able to perform various actions and it will contact the api server depending on the route of the web app   
  * Pythons app.route(someroute)  
  * I will need to design a simple pseudocode to help demonstrate how the system will handle and perform this  
  * This will include checking if in account creation process the password must meet specific requirements  
* The pseudocode below describes the backend login process/account creation process and how it is stored in the backend.

```sql
database = "app.db"

BEGIN registeruser(username,password)
		username = get input from client user
		password = get input from client user
		IF username does not exist in database THEN
			append username,password to database
			DISPLAY "account created"
			renderapp = redirect to homepage with login(username,password)
		ELSE
			DISPLAY "username taken!"
		ENDIF 
END registeruser(username,password)

BEGIN Login(username,password)
	username = get input from the client user
	password = get input from the client user
	User_id = username and password
	IF User_id does not match database records THEN
		DISPLAY "invalid username or password"
	ELSE
		renderapp = redirect to homepage with login(username,password)
		session_id = append user_id to local storage
	ENDIF
END Login(username,password)
```

### **Desk checks:**

Auth system (signup):

**Known values:** Jeff , JeffsVerySecurePassword  | Jeff, 123

| Username | Password | Conditon Username unique? | Database  | Output |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Jeff | JeffsVerySecurePassword | True | {Jeff , JeffsVerySecurePassword }  | “Account created” |
| Jeff | 123 | False |  | “Username taken\!” |

**Evaluation of test:** Success backend server logic works as intended and only injects data username is unique.  (algorithm works as intended)

	Auth system (login):

	Known values: Jeff , JeffsVerySecurePassword | Jeff , JustGiveMeAccess

| Username input | Password Input | Username and password match database record? | Output | Redirect to homepage |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Jeff | JeffsVerySecurePassword | True | “Logged in” | True |
| Jeff  | JustGiveMeAccess | False | “Invalid username or password” | False |

Evaluation: backend authentication is secure and only grants access to users registered in the database. (algorithm works as intended)

  * ![][image8]

  	

*  For signup:

# ***Data dictionary***

* Data dictionary includes key variables that store information/data  
* Backend (sqlite database table):

| Variable | Type | Description | Example |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Id  | string |  A primary key uniquely identifies the user through the use of unique user identification (uuid). Even if they change account username  | “a1b2c3d4e5f678901234567890abcdef” |
| username | string | Username of the user. | “Jeff” |
| Password  | string | Hashed/encrypted password of the user. | “$2b$12$5P/ui7eRT5NqgWOOP7Iu6u1bAB4zb3sYiG.N7ww9soksBVE9PBq9m” |

* Frontend:  
  * 

| Variable | Type | Description | Example |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | integer | Unique identifier for the resource.  | 1 |
| title | string | The title of the resource | “Introduction to Object-Oriented Programming” |
| description | string | A short description of resource | “Learn the basics of OOP including classes, objects” |
| category | string | The category of resource | “OOP” |
| difficulty | string | The level of difficulty of resource | “Beginner” |
| type | string | The type of resource | “Article” |
| tags | Array | A array list of tags in strings related to resource | \[“classes” , “objects”\]  |
| url | string | The url of the resource | “https://realpython.com/python3-object-oriented-programming/” |
| currentsection | string | Current section displayed | “home” |
| shouldfocus search | Booleon | If resource search should be focused | True |
| auth | JSON | Stores the auth user’s data | {"id":123,"username":"jeff"} |

# ***Development journal***

* Received the assessment task notification  
* Started planning on what to make for the PWA

**15/10/25:**

* Finished designing the top of the landing page

* Notes:

  * Background will be replaced with an interactive coding background that progresses as the user scrolls through the page (using GSAP) in production.

* Completed landing/homepage design:

* The final section's design is not particularly good; I might try to come up with an alternative way to display content more effectively.

**1710/25:**

* I wanted to get hands on experience with Svelte since I had done desinging the homepage of my pwa I tried to code it into my pwa to test how easy the design is to implement.  
* For my first attempt was a struggle I was able to map out the locations using the Inspect element (developer tools) to figure out the css positions and locations

**18/10/25:**

* Exported the figma design into css and html (recoded it in Typescript XML)  
  * [**used plugin**](https://www.figma.com/community/plugin/1421932899298722297/convert-figma-design-to-html-css)  
* The result was amazing and mostly accurate to my Canva design:  
* Used gsap to handle scroll based animations

  ![][image14]

* With the help of Tailwind I was able to use their provided examples to make the canva design into actual code which saved pain.  
  * This is just the frontend buttons do not work  
    * I need to code the backend for that.  
* Began designing other pages in figma.  
* Resource section looked like this:

* Concept of page

* Removed header looks cleaner:

* Got an error from deleting an important file:

* restored from git stash  
* Finished most of the user interface  
  * Tested user experience   
    * Decided side navigation was blocking some text on cards, which can lead to a bad user experience.  
      * I plan on adding a minimise button to allow the user to see more content on the page without the need to scroll.  
* Cleaned and optimised the projects code and structure:  
  * This caused an error:  
    * The navigation bars were not displaying at all

![][image19]

* Errors were shown in terminal I had to figure out why it was not showing  
* ![][image20]

![][image21]

* Error found : vite could not find button.tsx   
  * Solution options:  
    * 1: define the correct location by first specifying the global import (must edit config files for this)  
    * 2: move navigation.tsx to scope to be able to import the button without making it global.  
* Fixed error by defining the correct location and fixing imports  
  * Fixed by importing CSS the correct way (in index.css):  
* ![][image22]  
* Also remade home page section about the pwa:  
  ![][image23]

**21/10/25:**  
Added mobile navigation to app:  
![][image24]  
![][image25]

* **COMPLETED FRONTEND** 

**22/10/25:**

* Now I am done with frontend (react \+ vite) I need to start creating the backend  
  * This will include a login page where the user must enter username and password to gain access to this pwa, and a logout and delete account option.  
    * For the sake of this project this will be for local accounts (username and password) no OAuth through Google accounts, etc.  
  * Assessment states to use Flask so that I will stick with that   
    * Easiest to set up   
      * Good for HSC   
* Finished setting up the file structure and used automation to create a Python script that will automatically start backend and frontend, \+ install anything needed.  
* Using pseudocode to determine how I am going to design the backend systems:  
  * Removes the need to manually type  
  * Faster development environment  
    * Faster testing  
  * Easier for the client to use   
    * They just have to run 1 script.

  ![][image26]

* Also created separate GitHub branches in case something goes wrong  
  * I will be working on the backend on the backend branch   
    * Once the project is complete and no errors occur, the backend branch will be merged into the main branch, and project will be submitted.  
* Created a backend test script to see if my backend works:

![][image27]

* Hooked backend to frontend   
  * They can now request and send data to each other.  
  * Using vite server proxy.  
  * Tested if it works  
* Frontend fetch logic example:

![][image28]

* Got backend to create database if it does not exist with fields using sqlite \+ app secret key:

# ***Evidence of testing the solution***

**TESTING SERVER FOR CONNECTION TO THE CLIENT:**

* Case test server backend does not work:  
  * Exit code: **500 Internal Server Error**  
  * Reason: server not connected (server is offline frontend client failed to fetch JSON data from endpoint-api/test.  
    * Resulted in an error   
  * Results: successfully tested error handle and api if it is connected to the server or not (will be implemented in app for debug and feedback purposes).  
  * ![][image39]  
* Case test server backend connected  
  * Exit code: **200 (pass/success**)  
* Reason: backend server is up and running  
* Results: backend server successfully connected to the client and can send and receive payload requests  
  * ![][image40]  
* Lots of errors (syntax and rendering for onchange sections)  
* Developer note: **error**  
  * Application login and account registration does not function at all and falls to create an account.   
    * Extensive debugging for the backend needs to take place to hunt bugs and determine what is wrong.  
      * Done using exception handling to catch error point  
      * If backend code is the issue.  
      * Or if client request is the issue.  
  * **FIXED**: the client was the issue I was fetching from a api endpoint that did not even exist:

Tested sample success:  
![][image35]

Test case register:

* If user enters a invalid username/password it is not authorise them  
  * The network api tracer demonstrates this test below:

![][image41]

* If user enters valid password/username that matches database records it will authorise and redirect them:  
  * ![][image42]

Test case \- register:

* If user enters username that is already in database it should result in conflict and reject register in database: **(test success)**  
  ![][image43]  
* If user enters username that does not exist in database record it should add it:  
  ![][image44]

Test case \- performance:

Light house report:

![][image45]

* Better then most applications I tested on [github.com](http://github.com) , [docs.google.com](http://docs.google.com)  
* App uses react , animations which are the reason why performance score is 75  
  * Other test cases are good.

# ***Evaluation***

My progressive Web Application, Software Readvanced project met its main goal to support NSW Software Engineering students. The app allows users to access learning resources, view tutorials, and log in securely.

The final app works as intended. Navigation between pages is smooth, and it performs well across different screen sizes. The login and signup system works correctly, storing usernames and hashed passwords safely in the database.

The user interface is simple, modern, and responsive. The colour theme looks professional, using Tailwind CSS and GSAP for animations and styling. The resource cards and search bar make the app easy to use, while mobile navigation improves accessibility. Overall, I’m happy with how the design turned out.

I used React and Vite for the frontend and Flask with SQLite for the backend. Connecting both parts was challenging, but I learned how to send and receive data using API routes. Testing included checking login/signup functions, server connections, and invalid inputs. I used desk checks and fixed many errors during development, especially incorrect API routes. After debugging, everything worked as expected.

My time management was mostly good. I spent the first few days on Figma and Canva designs before moving to coding. I had to reduce the project scope by removing extra features like “delete account” to focus on finishing the core functions.

This project taught me a lot about full-stack development, debugging, and UI design. If I had more time, I would improve offline support, optimise animations, and add more resource features.

Overall, Software Readvanced is a successful learning project for my major project. It meets the requirements and works well as a learning resource for students.
